fields.description:
## Why
Audit OSS finding #2 (MEDIUM). The GitHub mirror has no CodeQL / SAST workflow. Security scanning today is gitleaks-driven secret detection on the Gerrit review path plus a gating pip-audit — good, but no static code analysis. CodeQL is free for public repos and runs fine on the read-only mirror (it only needs push/schedule triggers and writes results to the Security tab, not the tree), adding an independent detection layer that the audit's manual review cannot replicate continuously.

## What / files
Add `.github/workflows/codeql.yml`:
- Analyze `python` and `javascript-typescript` (the workflow-editor assets under src/).
- Triggers: `push` to `main` + a weekly `schedule` (cron).
- Least-privilege `permissions`: `security-events: write`, `contents: read`, `actions: read`.
- `concurrency` with `cancel-in-progress: true`.
- Pin actions **consistently with the existing repo convention**: the repo pins by MAJOR VERSION TAG (e.g. `actions/checkout@v5`, `actions/setup-python@v6` in test.yml/gerrit-verify.yaml), NOT by commit SHA. Use `github/codeql-action/*@v3` to match that convention (do not introduce SHA-pinning that no other workflow in this repo uses).
- Route alerts to the default Security tab.

## Scope
In: `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` (new file). Out: changing the pinning convention of other workflows; wiring alerts to anything other than the Security tab. NOTE on triage AC: CodeQL's first run executes on the GitHub mirror only AFTER this stacked change lands on main, so first-run finding triage happens post-landing (tracked, not a pre-close blocker in the worktree).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` exists and is valid YAML (actionlint / yaml parse clean), analyzing python + javascript-typescript
- [ ] `permissions` block is least-privilege (security-events: write, contents: read, actions: read); actions pinned by version tag matching repo convention (`@vN`)
- [ ] A weekly `schedule` trigger is configured in addition to `push` on main
- [ ] After the stack lands on main, the first CodeQL run is green and any true-positive findings are triaged (fixed or dismissed with reason)
